    at GateEditor (/app/gates/[id]/page.tsx:42:18)

patch.description:
Flips the triage state of one error — the only mutation this surface allows. Valid transitions are between `open`, `resolved`, and `ignored`; the body must carry exactly `{ "status": … }`.

A `resolved` error reopens automatically (ingestion-side) if it recurs; `ignored` is sticky until flipped back here. Returns the updated row.

**Use cases**

- **Triage** — `{ "status": "ignored" }` to suppress a known-benign issue from the open list.
- **Close out** — `{ "status": "resolved" }` once the fix lands; it reopens on its own if the error recurs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque error id (`err_…`).

resolve.description: Close the error once the fix is deployed. It reopens automatically if it recurs.

ignore.description: Suppress a known-benign error from the open list. Sticky until flipped back.

post.summary: File a feedback ticket for an error
post.description:
Files a feedback ticket (`type: "error"`) for a tracked production error — the "File an issue" action on the errors dashboard. The ticket carries the error's fingerprint as its `sourceRef` so it dedupes against, and joins back to, the tracked error. Takes no body.

Idempotent: if an open `error` ticket already tracks this fingerprint (hand- or auto-filed), that existing ticket is returned instead of creating a duplicate. Returns `404` if the error does not exist.

**Use case:** Promote a noisy tracked error into an actionable ticket in the ops queue (the same item the worker auto-files once an error crosses its occurrence threshold), so it can be triaged, assigned, and burned down via the `shipeasy-ops-work` skill.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque error id (`err_…`).

201.description: File a feedback ticket for an error

post.description:
Marks one tracked error `resolved` — the single-purpose "close out" action. Takes no body; it is `PATCH /api/admin/errors/{id}` pinned to `{ "status": "resolved" }`, exposed so tooling can close an error without being handed the full open/resolved/ignored status machine. A resolved error reopens automatically (ingestion-side) if it recurs, so resolving is always safe: a premature resolve un-does itself on the next occurrence. Returns the updated row; `404` if the error does not exist.

**Use case:** Close out a tracked error from an agent or script once its fix has shipped — e.g. after a deploy, resolve every open issue the change addressed and let recurrence reopen anything that wasn't actually fixed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque error id (`err_…`).

post.description:
Returns a bucketed occurrence timeseries for one tracked error (by its fingerprint), read from the `shipeasy_errors` Analytics Engine dataset (near-real-time; ingest lag is seconds). The window bounds are epoch **seconds**; `to` must be strictly greater than `from`. The response echoes the SQL that produced the rows.

**Use case:** Render the trend sparkline / occurrence chart on the error detail panel, or pull the raw bucketed counts to alert when an issue's rate spikes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque error id (`err_…`).

lastDayHourly.description: Hourly occurrence counts over a one-day window (bounds in epoch seconds).

example.sql:
SELECT intDiv(toUInt32(double2), 3600) * 3600 AS t,
  sum(double1 * _sample_interval) AS v
FROM shipeasy_errors
WHERE index1 = 'e976b15e-2f0d-4c6e-9b1a-3a7c1f2d8e90'
  AND blob1 = '9c1f4f1f2c0c4a5fa1c2b6d3e7c8e3a1'
  AND double2 >= 1751000400 AND double2 < 1751086800
GROUP BY t
ORDER BY t

get.description:
Returns every connector in the project as a bare array (no pagination envelope).

The encrypted credentials backing each connector are never serialised — only the connector's non-secret `config`, `accountLabel`, and last-attempt health (`lastError`, `lastAttemptAt`, `lastSuccessAt`) are returned.

**Use case:** Render the integrations/triggers settings page, or drive a CI check that asserts every `github` connector is `enabled` and last dispatched without error.

post.description:
Creates a connector. The request body is discriminated on `provider`.

- **OAuth/app providers** (`google_sheets`, `github`, `slack`) — supply `{ provider, name, events }`. The connector is created `enabled: false` with empty `config` and no credentials; the provider's OAuth flow then attaches credentials and enables it.
- **Trigger providers** (`claude_trigger`, `cursor_trigger`, `copilot_trigger`, `jules_trigger`) — supply `config` plus the provider's credential field(s) and the connector is fireable immediately. Trigger creates are **idempotent** by their natural key (`config.routineId` / `config.repoUrl` / `config.owner`+`config.repo` / `config.source`): re-creating updates the existing row rather than duplicating it.

**Use cases**

- **File bugs as GitHub Issues** — `{ "provider": "github", "name": "Bugs → acme/app", "events": ["bug.created"] }`, then finish the GitHub App install.
- **Nightly ops sweep** — register a `claude_trigger` with its `routineId` and (optionally) a fire `token`; subscribe `events` later to auto-fire on new bugs.
- **Cold cloud-agent run** — register a `cursor_trigger`/`jules_trigger` with the repo coordinates plus both keys, or a `copilot_trigger` with the repo + user PAT.

githubStub.description: Creates a disabled `github` connector subscribed to `bug.created`. Finish the GitHub App install to attach credentials and enable it.

claudeTrigger.description: Registers a Claude Code routine trigger with its fire token. Idempotent by `config.routineId`. `events` left empty so it does not auto-fire paid runs yet.

config.fireText: Burn down the open bug + feature-request queue.

cursorTrigger.description: Both keys required up front: the Cursor `apiKey` launches the run, the `opsKey` is injected as SHIPEASY_CLI_TOKEN. Idempotent by `config.repoUrl`.

get.description:
Returns a single connector by id. The encrypted credentials are never serialised.

**Use case:** Inspect one connector's health — its `enabled` state, subscribed `events`, and last-attempt outcome (`lastError`/`lastSuccessAt`).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id.

patch.description:
Partial update — only supplied fields change. `events` and `config` **replace** wholesale; there is no merge or append. Secrets cannot be set through this endpoint (credential rotation goes through the provider-specific re-register flow).

The response carries only `{ id }` — re-fetch via `GET /api/admin/connectors/{id}` for the new row.

**Use cases**

- **Pause a connector** — `{ "enabled": false }`. Stops all dispatch / auto-fire without deleting it.
- **Change subscribed events** — send the full new `events` array. An empty array unsubscribes the connector from every event.
- **Rename** — `{ "name": "Bugs → acme/app issues" }`.
- **Retarget** — send a new `config` (e.g. a different Sheets `sheetTitle`); it replaces the stored config wholesale.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id.

pause.description: Stops all dispatch / auto-fire. Re-enable with `{ "enabled": true }`.

resubscribe.description: Array fields replace wholesale. Send the full new list; an empty array unsubscribes from everything.

retarget.description: Replaces the stored non-secret config wholesale.

delete.description:
Permanently removes the connector and its stored credentials.

**Use case:** Disconnect an integration for good — e.g. tear down a `github` connector after migrating bug routing elsewhere.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id.

post.description:
Manually kicks a trigger connector's run — Claude (kicks its preconfigured routine) or Cursor / Copilot / Jules (launches a cold cloud-agent run). Firing is event-less: it kicks the run with an optional caller-supplied prompt override rather than dispatching a single lifecycle payload.

Only trigger providers can be fired, and only once authenticated (a tokenless trigger cannot fire). The attempt's outcome is recorded on the connector's `lastAttemptAt` / `lastError` / `lastSuccessAt`.

**Use case:** Kick a one-off ops sweep on demand from the dashboard's "Fire now" button, optionally overriding the routine's default prompt.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id.

default.summary: Fire with the provider default prompt
default.description: Empty body — kicks the run with the provider's own default (Claude → configured `fireText`, others → built cold-start prompt).

value.text: Triage only the P0 bugs reported in the last 24h.

200.description: The trigger run was successfully kicked off.

post.description:
Dispatches a single synthetic `bug.created` payload to the connector's destination so you can verify the integration end-to-end. Unlike `fire`, this runs the real dispatch path (posts a Slack message / appends a Sheets row / files a GitHub Issue) with throwaway test content. The attempt's outcome is recorded on the connector's `lastAttemptAt` / `lastError` / `lastSuccessAt`.

When the provider produces a linkable artifact (e.g. a GitHub Issue), its URL is returned as `issueUrl`; otherwise `issueUrl` is `null`.

**Use case:** Click "Send test" after wiring up a connector to confirm credentials and config are correct before relying on it for real events.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id.

200.description: The test dispatch reached the destination.

patch.description:
Edit an existing coding-agent **trigger** connector (`claude_trigger` / `cursor_trigger` / `copilot_trigger` / `jules_trigger`) — replace its non-secret `config` and, optionally, rotate its credential secret(s). Discriminated on `provider`; the id in the path selects the connector and its `provider` must match the body.

Unlike the generic `PATCH /api/admin/connectors/{id}` (which cannot touch secrets), this endpoint always replaces the non-secret `config` wholesale and merges any supplied secret over the stored credential cipher — so a single half of a two-key pair (e.g. just the ops key) can be rotated on its own. A blank/omitted secret leaves the stored cipher untouched.

The response carries only `{ id }` — re-fetch via `GET /api/admin/connectors/{id}` for the new row.

**Use case:** Re-point a Cursor trigger at a new repo ref, or rotate a Claude trigger's fire token, without deleting and re-creating the connector.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque connector id. Its stored `provider` must match the body's `provider`.

claude.description: Replaces the routine config and rotates the fire token.

cursor.summary: Cursor — rotate just the ops key
cursor.description: Leaves the launch key untouched, rotates only the restricted ops key.

post.description:
Creates (or idempotently updates) a coding-agent **trigger** connector — the recurring, unattended run that burns down the ops queue in `--pr` mode. Discriminated on `provider`; only the four Shipeasy-fireable providers are accepted (`claude_trigger`, `cursor_trigger`, `copilot_trigger`, `jules_trigger`). Config + credential(s) arrive together; creates are idempotent by the provider's natural key, so re-creating updates the existing row.

Platforms without a fire endpoint (Codex, Windsurf, Cline, OpenClaw, OpenCode, Continue) cannot be created here — they are scheduled on their own platform (typically a GitHub Actions `schedule:` cron running the platform's headless CLI with the trigger prompt).

**Use cases**

- **Register a Claude routine** — after `RemoteTrigger {action:"create"}` returns `trig_…`, `{ "provider": "claude_trigger", "config": { "routineId": "trig_…" } }` (tokenless is fine; add the fire token later).
- **Cold Cursor/Jules run** — repo coordinates + both keys; Shipeasy launches the run and the PR opens via the provider's GitHub App.
- **Copilot cloud agent** — repo + a Copilot-licensed user PAT with the "Agent tasks" permission.

x-cli.helpPreamble:
A trigger is an unattended, scheduled agent run: on a cron cadence it runs the
shipeasy-ops-work loop in --pr mode against your project — one atomic diff and
ONE pull request per fixed item, nothing auto-merges.

Whatever the provider, the scheduled run executes this trigger prompt (headless
CLIs take it as the prompt; cloud agents as the task body):

  You are an unattended Shipeasy maintenance run. Authenticate every `shipeasy`
  call with these env vars (the CLI reads them directly — do NOT run
  `shipeasy login`, never echo the token). Put them at the top of EVERY shell
  invocation that calls `shipeasy`; each command runs in a fresh shell:

  export SHIPEASY_CLI_TOKEN="<OPS_KEY>"
  export SHIPEASY_PROJECT_ID="<PROJECT_ID>"

  Ensure the repo is bound: test -f .shipeasy || printf '{"project_id":"<PROJECT_ID>"}\n' > .shipeasy
  (never commit .shipeasy).

  Refresh to the latest plugin + CLI, then run the ops work loop in --pr mode:
    <PLUGIN-INSTALL-FOR-THIS-HOST>       # plugin install for claude/copilot, or `npx -y skills add …`
    npm install -g @shipeasy/cli@latest
  Then follow the installed shipeasy-ops-work (--pr) workflow verbatim: burn down
  the queue, one atomic diff per item, open ONE PR per item from a safe branch
  prefix, flip each to ready_for_qa, add "Closes #<issue>" where an item has a
  connected GitHub issue. If the queue is empty, exit cleanly. Never merge.

<OPS_KEY> is a restricted `ops` key — mint with
`npx -y @shipeasy/cli@latest keys create --type ops`. It reads the queue, flips
status, links the PR it opens, and creates resources only; it can never edit or
delete existing resources, and auto-extends its 7-day expiry on each run.
<PROJECT_ID> comes from the bound .shipeasy. Never print either value.

Safety: auto-approve flags (--approval-mode=yolo, --dangerously-skip-permissions,
…) remove the human gate — run only in an isolated env. Unattended loops spend
tokens/credits on a cadence: start weekly/daily and watch the first runs.

commands.name: create claude
commands.summary: Register a Claude Code scheduled routine as the trigger connector
commands.help:
The reference implementation — a routine in Anthropic's cloud, no GitHub
Actions. The routines API is reachable only by the agent's in-process
RemoteTrigger tool, so provisioning is a hybrid split:

1. `shipeasy ops trigger prep --frequency <4h|6h|daily|weekdays|weekly|cron>`
   mints the restricted ops key, resolves repo + cron, and writes the exact
   RemoteTrigger create body to a 0600 temp file (the key is never printed).
2. The agent picks an environment_id from its RemoteTrigger env list — the
   environment's network access must allow shipeasy.ai + api.shipeasy.ai
   (Custom network; new envs default to Trusted, which fails every shipeasy
   call) — then calls RemoteTrigger {action:"create"} and captures trig_….
   Verify with {action:"run"} (confirm first — it spends tokens and may open
   a real PR).
3. Register the connector (idempotent by routineId; tokenless is fine):

     shipeasy ops trigger create claude --config '{"routineId":"trig_…"}'

The fire token ("Fire now" + event auto-fire) is web-UI-only: open
https://claude.ai/code/routines → the routine → add an API trigger →
Generate token, then re-run the create with --token <token> and optionally
--events '["bug.created","feature_request.created"]' — the same row is
upgraded in place. GitHub access is connected once via /web-setup or the
Claude GitHub App. Manage/pause the routine at claude.ai/code/routines.

commands.name: create cursor
commands.summary: Register a Cursor cloud-agent trigger (cold-fire; Shipeasy launches the run)
commands.help:
Cursor cloud agents are cold-fireable: one authenticated POST to
https://api.cursor.com/v1/agents starts a run from nothing, and
autoCreatePR opens the PR via Cursor's GitHub App. Registering the
connector stores both credentials, so Shipeasy can fire it on demand and
on subscribed events; the ops key rides the launch envVars (never the
prompt):

  shipeasy ops trigger create cursor \
    --config '{"repoUrl":"https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>","projectId":"<prj_…>"}' \
    --api-key <CURSOR_API_KEY> --ops-key <ops_…>

For a fixed cadence (nightly runs without new feedback) add a Cursor
Automation (cursor.com/automations) with the same trigger prompt — cron +
"Pull request creation" enabled. Note: Automations always run in Max Mode
(billed on cloud-agent usage).

commands.name: create copilot
commands.summary: Register a GitHub Copilot cloud-agent trigger
commands.help:
Copilot's cloud agent starts from one authenticated POST to
https://api.github.com/agents/repos/<owner>/<repo>/tasks with
{ prompt, create_pull_request: true }. Auth is a user-to-server token
ONLY: a fine-grained user PAT with the "Agent tasks" repo permission
(read+write) for a Copilot-licensed account — the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN and
GitHub App installation tokens are rejected. The agent reads
SHIPEASY_CLI_TOKEN from the repo's own Agents secret store, so Shipeasy
stores only the PAT:

  shipeasy ops trigger create copilot \
    --config '{"owner":"<owner>","repo":"<repo>"}' --token <fine-grained PAT>

Set SHIPEASY_CLI_TOKEN (the restricted ops key) + SHIPEASY_PROJECT_ID in
the repo's Agents secrets first. For a fixed cadence use Copilot
automations (repo Agents tab; hourly/daily/weekly, private/internal repos
only) or a scheduled Actions job running `gh agent-task create`.

commands.name: create jules
commands.help:
Jules — Google's async coding agent (the fireable Gemini path) — starts a
session from one POST to https://jules.googleapis.com/v1alpha/sessions
(header X-Goog-Api-Key) with sourceContext.source =
sources/github/<owner>/<repo> and automationMode: "AUTO_CREATE_PR". Jules
exposes no env channel, so the restricted ops key is embedded in the
session prompt (safe — it is the limited ops key):

  shipeasy ops trigger create jules \
    --config '{"source":"sources/github/<owner>/<repo>"}' \
    --api-key <JULES_API_KEY> --ops-key <ops_…>

Connect the repo to Jules once (jules.google.com → connect GitHub). For a
fixed cadence use Jules scheduled tasks (UI-only), or fall back to the
Gemini CLI in a GitHub Actions schedule: job
(`gemini -p "<TRIGGER PROMPT>" --approval-mode=yolo`; auth GEMINI_API_KEY).

fallback.help:
Direct (Shipeasy-fired) triggers are not supported for this platform — it
exposes no HTTP "start a run" endpoint our Worker can call. Supported here:
claude, cursor, copilot, jules (alias: gemini) — see
`shipeasy ops trigger create <provider> --help`.

Platform-scheduled setup instead (the platform's own scheduler fires the
run; nothing to register in Shipeasy):

1. Mint the restricted ops key (`shipeasy sdk keys create --type ops --json`)
   and read the project id from .shipeasy. Never print either.
2. Build the trigger prompt (run `shipeasy ops trigger create claude --help`
   for the shared template) with the key, project id, and your platform's
   install line substituted.
3. Schedule a headless run with it — a GitHub Actions `schedule:` cron
   (always-on; credentials as repo secrets incl. a GH_TOKEN/PAT with
   contents:write + pull-requests:write) or a local cron/daemon (machine
   must stay awake):

     codex     GitHub Actions cron → `codex cloud exec --env <ENV_ID> "<PROMPT>"`
               (submits a Codex Cloud task — machine-off; configure network +
               SHIPEASY_* secrets once at chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/environments)
     windsurf  Devin Scheduled Sessions (cloud cron; WINDSURF/DEVIN API key)
     cline     `cline schedule create --cron "…" --prompt "<PROMPT>"` (local daemon)
     openclaw  `openclaw cron create "…" "<PROMPT>"` (local gateway; static API key)
     opencode  `opencode run "<PROMPT>"` + external cron (permission: "allow")
     continue  `cn -p --auto "<PROMPT>"` + external cron (CONTINUE_API_KEY)

4. Verify one manual fire (expect a PR or a clean empty-queue exit) and
   manage/pause it on that platform's scheduler.

get.description:
Returns a single page of the project's API keys ordered by `created_at desc, id desc`, in the standard `{ data, next_cursor }` envelope. Use the `cursor` query parameter to paginate.

Response fields are **snake_case** (`created_at`, `revoked_at`, `created_by_email`, `last4`). The raw token is never returned — only its `last4` tail, so a held key can be matched against the masked row. Revoked keys are included (with a non-null `revoked_at`).

**Use case:** Audit which keys exist for a project — surface stale or never-expiring keys, or drive a CI check that asserts no `client` key is still active in `prod` after a rotation.

post.description:
Mints a new API key and returns the plaintext token **once** — it is stored hashed and can never be retrieved again, so capture it on creation.

Only `type` is required. `env` is **required** for `server` and `client` keys (the key is bound to one environment, which is the read-env isolation boundary); for `admin` and `ops` keys `env` is ignored and the key is pinned to `prod`. Expiry is fixed for some types: `admin` keys always get a 90-day expiry and `ops` keys a short sliding window, regardless of `expiresInDays`. Only `server`/`client` keys count toward the plan key limit.

**Use cases**

- **Back-end key** — `{ "type": "server", "env": "prod" }` for the production server SDK.
- **Public browser key** — `{ "type": "client", "env": "prod", "name": "marketing site" }` to embed in the browser SDK.
- **Scoped, expiring key** — `{ "type": "server", "env": "staging", "scopes": ["gates:evaluate"], "expiresInDays": 30 }` for a time-boxed integration.

serverKey.summary: Server key — bound to an environment
serverKey.description: A back-end SDK key. `env` is required for `server`/`client` keys.

scopedExpiring.description: A time-boxed key limited to gate evaluation, expiring in 30 days.

opsKey.description: The unattended-trigger credential. `env` is ignored (pinned to `prod`); expiry is a short sliding window.

post.description:
Revokes a key by id — stamps its `revoked_at` and deletes the hot-path KV entry so the token stops authenticating immediately. Takes no body.

Idempotent: revoking an already-revoked key is a no-op and returns the same `{ id, revoked: true }`. Returns `404` if no such key exists in the project.

**Use case:** Rotate a leaked or stale credential — mint the replacement, then revoke the old key.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Stable opaque key id (UUID) returned by `create` / `list`.

get.description:
Universal substring search across the project's primary resources — feature gates, experiments, dynamic configs, killswitches, metrics, and team members. Matches the trimmed `q` against each resource's immutable `name` and its human title/display name (members match on display name or email), newest-first, capped per resource family so one noisy type can't crowd out the rest.

Returns a flat, ranked-by-type list of hits (members first); an empty or whitespace-only `q` returns no hits. Each hit carries a project-relative `href` that deep-links to the row.

**Use case:** Back a command-palette / quick-switcher in the dashboard, or look up the id of a resource by a fragment of its name before driving another API call.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Search query. Matched as a case-insensitive substring against each resource's `name` and title/display name. Trimmed; empty returns no hits.

bearerSdkKey.description: Pass an admin SDK key as `Authorization: Bearer sdk_admin_…`. Mint via `POST /api/admin/keys` with `type: "admin"`.

ProjectId.name: X-Project-Id
ProjectId.description: Project the request operates on. Optional — defaults to the project the SDK key belongs to; pass it only to scope a multi-project key (the generated client sets it once from its configuration, so per-call callers never thread it).

PaginationCursor.name: cursor
PaginationCursor.description: Opaque cursor returned in the previous page's `next_cursor`. Omit for the first page.

Q.name: q
Q.description: Case-insensitive substring filter across the resource's human-readable text columns (e.g. `name`, `title`, `description`). OR-matched across those columns; omit to return everything.

name.description: Stable gate key used by SDKs (`Shipeasy.checkGate(user, '<name>')`). Single segment or `folder.name`. Lowercase letters, digits, `_` or `-`; max 128 chars. Immutable after create — rename = delete + recreate.

enabled.description: Master switch. Returned as `1`/`0` from D1; treat truthy as enabled.

type.description: Gate kind — `targeting` (normal flag) or `holdout` (restricted public % + whitelist).

attr.description: Attribute key on the evaluation context (e.g. `country`, `plan`, `email`). Matched case-sensitively against `Shipeasy.checkGate(user, name)` input.

op.description: Comparison operator. Equality: `eq`/`neq`. Set membership: `in`/`not_in` (value is an array). Numeric order: `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`. Semver order: `semver_gt`/`semver_gte`/`semver_lt`/`semver_lte` (value is a dotted version like `4.2.0`). String: `contains` (substring), `regex` (JS-flavour pattern, value is the pattern string). Cross-resource references (evaluated only on the worker hot path, where both KV blobs load): `gate_pass` (`value` is another gate's name — true when that gate passes for the same caller); `exp_in` (`attr` is a running experiment's name, `value` is a group name, `"$holdout"`, or `"$any"` — true when the caller is in that variant / the universe holdout / any variant).

value.description: Operand. Shape depends on `op`: scalar for `eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte/contains/semver_*`; array of scalars for `in/not_in`; string pattern for `regex`; referenced gate name for `gate_pass`; group name / `"$holdout"` / `"$any"` for `exp_in`.

items.description: Single targeting predicate. A gate's `rules` array is evaluated with AND semantics; use the gatekeeper `stack` for OR / fall-through logic.

salt.description: Hash salt used for percentage bucketing.

groupName.description: Group label (note: `groupName` in response, `group` in request).

ownerEmail.description: Owner email (note: `ownerEmail` in response, `owner_email` in request).

id.description: Client-supplied stable id for the stack entry (used for React keys, audit diffs, and as the default per-condition rollout salt).

type.description: Discriminator. `condition` = rule-based predicate sub-gate.

name.description: Human label shown in the dashboard.

fromTemplate.description: Id of the project template this entry was seeded from, or `null` if hand-authored.

rules.description: Predicates evaluated under `pass`. Empty array = match nothing.

attr.description: Attribute key on the evaluation context (e.g. `country`, `plan`, `email`). Matched case-sensitively against `Shipeasy.checkGate(user, name)` input.

op.description: Comparison operator. Equality: `eq`/`neq`. Set membership: `in`/`not_in` (value is an array). Numeric order: `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`. Semver order: `semver_gt`/`semver_gte`/`semver_lt`/`semver_lte` (value is a dotted version like `4.2.0`). String: `contains` (substring), `regex` (JS-flavour pattern, value is the pattern string). Cross-resource references (evaluated only on the worker hot path, where both KV blobs load): `gate_pass` (`value` is another gate's name — true when that gate passes for the same caller); `exp_in` (`attr` is a running experiment's name, `value` is a group name, `"$holdout"`, or `"$any"` — true when the caller is in that variant / the universe holdout / any variant).

value.description: Operand. Shape depends on `op`: scalar for `eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte/contains/semver_*`; array of scalars for `in/not_in`; string pattern for `regex`; referenced gate name for `gate_pass`; group name / `"$holdout"` / `"$any"` for `exp_in`.

items.description: Single targeting predicate. A gate's `rules` array is evaluated with AND semantics; use the gatekeeper `stack` for OR / fall-through logic.

rolloutPct.description: Per-condition rollout in basis points. After the rules match, the caller is bucketed at this %; in-bucket → gate `true`, otherwise fall through to the next entry. Absent ⇒ 10000 (100%, every matching caller passes).

bucketBy.description: Attribute used to hash the caller into the per-condition bucket. Default `userID`.

salt.description: Per-condition hash salt. Defaults to the entry `id`, then the gate salt.

from.description: Start of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

to.description: End of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

ramp.description: Linear rollout ramp. The effective rollout % interpolates `from`→`to` over `[startAt, startAt+durationMs]`, clamped outside that window, evaluated against the wall-clock at eval time. Overrides the entry's static `rolloutPct` while present.

locked.description: If `true`, dashboard hides edit controls (e.g. the trailing public-floor entry).

oneOf.description: Rule-based sub-gate. When the rules match and the caller falls in the per-condition rollout bucket, evaluation short-circuits and the gate returns `true`; otherwise it falls through to the next entry.

id.description: Client-supplied stable id for the stack entry.

type.description: Discriminator. `rollout` = percentage-bucket sub-gate.

name.description: Human label shown in the dashboard.

rolloutPct.description: Bucket size in basis points (0–10000 = 0%–100%). `5000` = 50%, `100` = 1%, allows sub-1% precision.

bucketBy.description: Attribute used to hash the caller into a bucket. Defaults to `user.userID`. Use e.g. `accountID` to bucket whole accounts together.

salt.description: Per-entry hash salt. Defaults to the gate's salt. Set explicitly to keep two rollouts independent or correlated across gates.

from.description: Start of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

to.description: End of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

ramp.description: Linear rollout ramp. The effective rollout % interpolates `from`→`to` over `[startAt, startAt+durationMs]`, clamped outside that window, evaluated against the wall-clock at eval time. Overrides the entry's static `rolloutPct` while present.

oneOf.description: Percentage-rollout sub-gate. Hashes `bucketBy` (default `userID`) with `salt` and returns `true` for `rolloutPct/10000` fraction of callers (or the ramp's effective % when `ramp` is set).

items.description: One entry in the ordered gatekeeper stack. Evaluated top-to-bottom; first matching entry decides. Mix `condition` and `rollout` entries to express `internal-only` + `1% beta` + `50% public` in a single gate.

creatorEmail.description: Resolved email of the user who created the gate (`created_by` → `users.email`), or `null` when the creator is unknown.

updaterEmail.description: Resolved email of the user who last edited the gate (`updated_by` → `users.email`), or `null` when the last editor is unknown.

version.description: Monotonic edit counter — starts at 1 on create (D1 `NOT NULL DEFAULT 1`) and increments on every `PATCH /api/admin/gates/{id}`.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 creation timestamp, or `null` on legacy rows created before the column existed.

GateApiRow.description: The single wire shape for a gate row, shared by `ListGatesResponse` (`data[]` items) and `GET /api/admin/gates/{id}`.

ErrorCode.description: Stable, machine-readable error code. Mirrors the `ErrorCode` catalogue in `@shipeasy/core`. Each operation lists the subset it can return under `x-error-codes`.

x-enum-descriptions.BAD_REQUEST: Malformed request (bad JSON, missing project scope).
x-enum-descriptions.UNAUTHORIZED: Missing or invalid admin SDK key.
x-enum-descriptions.FORBIDDEN: Key is valid but not allowed to act on this project.
x-enum-descriptions.PLAN_REQUIRED: The requested feature requires a higher plan tier (e.g. sequential testing, custom alpha, holdout, group count).
x-enum-descriptions.NOT_FOUND: The resource does not exist or is not visible to the caller.
x-enum-descriptions.ALREADY_EXISTS: A resource with this name already exists in the project.
x-enum-descriptions.INVALID_TRANSITION: The requested lifecycle transition is not allowed from the current state.
x-enum-descriptions.IMMUTABLE_FIELD: A field that is immutable in the current state was modified (e.g. editing allocation while running).
x-enum-descriptions.READ_ONLY: The resource is a read-only built-in (e.g. a built-in gate-rule template) and cannot be modified or deleted.
x-enum-descriptions.REFERENCED_IN_USE: The resource cannot be archived/deleted while another resource still references it.
x-enum-descriptions.VALIDATION: The request body failed structural (schema) validation.
x-enum-descriptions.REFERENCED_NOT_FOUND: A referenced entity (universe, metric, gate, event) does not exist.
x-enum-descriptions.GROUPS_WEIGHT_SUM: Experiment group weights must sum to 10000 (basis points).
x-enum-descriptions.EVENT_PENDING: The referenced event is still pending review and cannot back a metric yet.
x-enum-descriptions.PLAN_LIMIT: The action would exceed a plan quota (e.g. the tier's maximum experiments, metrics, or configs). Upgrade the plan or archive an existing resource.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_NO_GOAL_METRIC: The experiment has no goal metric set, which the requested action requires.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_ARCHIVED_RESTART: An archived experiment cannot be restarted.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_RESTORE_INVALID: The experiment cannot be restored from its current state.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_NOT_RUNNING: The action requires the experiment to be running.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_RUNNING_ARCHIVE: A running experiment cannot be archived — stop it first.
x-enum-descriptions.EXPERIMENT_IMMUTABLE_FIELD: A field that is immutable while the experiment is running was modified.
x-enum-descriptions.METRIC_NOT_FOUND: The referenced metric does not exist or is not visible to the caller.
x-enum-descriptions.METRIC_UNKNOWN_ID: The supplied metric id is malformed or not recognised.
x-enum-descriptions.AGENT_NOT_CONNECTED: The named AI agent type has no connected trigger connector in this project — list the available agents (`ops agents list` / `GET /api/admin/agent-profiles`) and use one of those, or connect the agent under Settings → Triggers.

Error.description: Uniform error envelope returned by every admin endpoint on a non-2xx status. `error` is the human-readable message; `code` is the stable machine code (present whenever the failure maps to a catalogued `ErrorCode`); `detail` carries extra context (validation paths, conflicting field); `fields` maps each failing input field to its own message; `instructions` is optional actionable guidance for resolving the error; `schema` echoes back the expected JSON Schema when a value failed schema validation.

detail.description: Extra context (validation details, conflicting field, referenced row).

fields.description: Per-field validation messages, keyed by the submitted field's path (dot-notation for nested fields, e.g. `groups.0.weight`). Present when structural validation failed on one or more specific input fields, so a client form can attach each message to its own input instead of collapsing everything into one banner.

instructions.description: Actionable guidance for resolving this error — what to check or change next.

schema.description: The expected JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) the submitted value must satisfy. Returned only when a value failed schema validation (e.g. a config `value` that does not match its config's saved schema), so the caller can correct the value and retry without re-fetching.

Folder.description: Optional folder name grouping items in the dashboard. Alphanumeric, `_` or `-` (no `/`). Part of the SDK lookup key (`<folder>/<name>`).

name.description: Stable gate key used by SDKs (`Shipeasy.checkGate(user, '<name>')`). Single segment or `folder.name`. Lowercase letters, digits, `_` or `-`; max 128 chars. Immutable after create — rename = delete + recreate.

type.description: Gate kind. `targeting` (default) is a normal flag with the full builder. `holdout` is a **restricted** flag — only a public rollout % and a whitelist are allowed; attribute rules and a gatekeeper stack are rejected. Used as an experiment's `holdout_gate`.

enabled.description: Master switch. Defaults to `true`. Set `false` to create the gate disabled (evaluates to `false` regardless of rules/rollout); flip on via `POST /{id}/enable` or PATCH.

rollout_pct.description: Initial rollout in **basis points** (0–10000 = 0%–100%) — `100` here means **1%**, not 100%. Use `rollout_percent` (0–100) below if you'd rather think in percent. Use `0` to create the gate dark and ramp via PATCH after deploy validation.

rollout_percent.description: Initial rollout as a **percentage** (0–100, fractional ok). Friendlier alias for `rollout_pct`; converted internally to basis points (e.g. `100` here = 10000 bp = 100%). If both `rollout_pct` and `rollout_percent` are set, `rollout_percent` wins.

rules.description: Targeting predicates. AND-combined. If non-empty, the gate returns `true` only for callers that satisfy every rule **and** fall under `rollout_pct`.

attr.description: Attribute key on the evaluation context (e.g. `country`, `plan`, `email`). Matched case-sensitively against `Shipeasy.checkGate(user, name)` input.

op.description: Comparison operator. Equality: `eq`/`neq`. Set membership: `in`/`not_in` (value is an array). Numeric order: `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`. Semver order: `semver_gt`/`semver_gte`/`semver_lt`/`semver_lte` (value is a dotted version like `4.2.0`). String: `contains` (substring), `regex` (JS-flavour pattern, value is the pattern string). Cross-resource references (evaluated only on the worker hot path, where both KV blobs load): `gate_pass` (`value` is another gate's name — true when that gate passes for the same caller); `exp_in` (`attr` is a running experiment's name, `value` is a group name, `"$holdout"`, or `"$any"` — true when the caller is in that variant / the universe holdout / any variant).

value.description: Operand. Shape depends on `op`: scalar for `eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte/contains/semver_*`; array of scalars for `in/not_in`; string pattern for `regex`; referenced gate name for `gate_pass`; group name / `"$holdout"` / `"$any"` for `exp_in`.

items.description: Single targeting predicate. A gate's `rules` array is evaluated with AND semantics; use the gatekeeper `stack` for OR / fall-through logic.

salt.description: Hash salt for percentage bucketing. Auto-generated if omitted. Provide explicitly to keep a gate's buckets stable across delete/recreate. **Immutable after create** — there is no PATCH for `salt` because changing it would re-bucket every caller.

stack.description: Optional gatekeeper stack. When provided, takes precedence over `rules` + `rollout_pct` at evaluation time. Omit (or pass `null`) for a flat gate.

id.description: Client-supplied stable id for the stack entry (used for React keys, audit diffs, and as the default per-condition rollout salt).

type.description: Discriminator. `condition` = rule-based predicate sub-gate.

name.description: Human label shown in the dashboard.

fromTemplate.description: Id of the project template this entry was seeded from, or `null` if hand-authored.

attr.description: Attribute key on the evaluation context (e.g. `country`, `plan`, `email`). Matched case-sensitively against `Shipeasy.checkGate(user, name)` input.

bucketBy.description: Attribute used to hash the caller into the per-condition bucket. Default `userID`.

salt.description: Per-condition hash salt. Defaults to the entry `id`, then the gate salt.

from.description: Start of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

to.description: End of the ramp in basis points (0–10000).

UpdateGateRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/admin/gates/{id}`. Partial — only supplied fields change. Array fields (`rules`, `stack`) replace, not merge.

UpdateGateResponse.description: Update returns only the id — re-fetch via `GET /api/admin/gates` for the full row.

ListGateActivityResponse.description: Recent audit rows for one gate, newest first.

name.description: Stable experiment key. Single segment or `folder.name` (a-z, 0-9, `_`/`-`; max 128 chars). Used by SDKs as `Shipeasy.getExperiment(user, '<name>')`. Immutable after create.

anyOf.description: User-attribute name used as the bucketing key — e.g. `user_id` (default), `session_id`, `device_id`, or a custom attribute like `company_id` to keep a whole org on one variant. Defaults to `user_id` when omitted.

universe.description: Universe name this experiment draws from.

holdoutGate.description: Per-experiment holdout gate name (a `holdout`-type flag), or `null`.

reservedHeadroom.description: Basis points of the split reserved for appended variants (group weights sum to 10000 − this).

hashVersion.description: Bucketing hash algorithm version for the experiment's pool slice (§B4). Defaults to 1.

poolOffsetBp.description: Basis-point offset of the experiment's contiguous slice in the universe pool, or `null` before a slice is allocated.

poolSizeBp.description: Basis-point width of the experiment's pool slice (equal to its allocation), or `null` before a slice is allocated.

name.description: Group label (e.g. `control`, `treatment_a`). Max 64 chars.

weight.description: Allocation weight in basis points (0–10000). The sum across all groups must equal exactly **10000** (100%).

params.description: Per-group parameter values delivered to the SDK when a caller is hashed into this group. Keys must match the experiment's `params` schema.

items.description: One variant in the experiment. Weights across all groups must sum to 10000.

startedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the experiment last transitioned to `running`, or `null`.

version.description: Save counter (number of published edits), or `null` on pre-versioning rows.

creatorEmail.description: Resolved creator email (`created_by` → users). Enriched field: present on list rows, omitted from the by-id detail.

updaterEmail.description: Resolved last-editor email (`updated_by` → users). Enriched field: present on list rows, omitted from the by-id detail.

verdict.description: Answer-first decision from the goal metric + guardrails + SRM vs. the significance threshold and min runtime: `ship`, `hold`, `wait`, `invalid`, `draft`. Enriched field: list rows only.

verdictTitle.description: Expanded verdict headline the results hero renders. Enriched field: list rows only.

verdictWhy.description: Verdict rationale the results hero renders. Enriched field: list rows only.

goalMetric.description: The experiment's goal metric `{ id, name }`, or `null` when none is set. Enriched field: list rows only.

guardrails.description: Guardrail metrics `{ id, name, eventName? }`. Enriched field: list rows only.

guardrailCount.description: Number of guardrail metrics. Enriched field: list rows only.

primaryLiftPct.description: Goal-metric lift of the leading variant vs. control, in percent, or `null` when not yet computed. Enriched field: list rows only.

significancePct.description: Statistical significance (1 − p) as a percent, or `null` when not yet computed. Enriched field: list rows only.

sampleSize.description: Total distinct units analysed, or `null` when not yet computed. Enriched field: list rows only.

exposure.description: Cumulative unique-users-enrolled series for the exposure sparkline. Enriched field: list rows only.

exposureTotal.description: Total distinct units ever exposed (sparkline headline), or `null`. Enriched field: list rows only.

MetricName.description: Stable metric key. Single segment or `folder.name`; lowercase letters, digits, `_`/`-`; max 128 chars.

query.description: Metric DSL string. Provide this OR `event`.

event.description: Event name to build the metric from server-side. Auto-created if missing.

aggregation.description: Reducer for the `event` form. Defaults to `count_users`.

value.description: Numeric event property for `sum`/`avg` (with `event`).

min_effect_of_interest.description: Per-experiment override of the metric's default minimum effect of interest (relative, 0–1) — the smallest change worth acting on for this experiment's decision. `null`/omitted inherits the metric default. For a guardrail, the non-inferiority margin.

ExperimentInlineMetric.description: Inline metric — a DSL `query`, or an `event` (+ `aggregation`/`value`) the server compiles into one.

name.description: Stable experiment key. Single segment or `folder.name` (a-z, 0-9, `_`/`-`; max 128 chars). Used by SDKs as `Shipeasy.getExperiment(user, '<name>')`. Immutable after create.

description.description: Free-form description. Max 2000 chars, markdown rendered in the dashboard.

hypothesis.description: Hypothesis statement shown in the editor. Display-only.

tag.description: Short tag chip rendered next to the name. Display-only.

audience.description: Audience label shown in the editor. Display-only.

anyOf.description: User-attribute name used as the bucketing key — e.g. `user_id` (default), `session_id`, `device_id`, or a custom attribute like `company_id` to keep a whole org on one variant. Defaults to `user_id` when omitted.

universe.description: Name of an existing universe in the project. Returns `422` if the universe doesn't exist.

targeting_gate.description: Optional gate name (a `targeting`-type flag). Only callers that pass the gate are enrolled in the experiment.

holdout_gate.description: Optional per-experiment holdout gate — the name of a `holdout`-type flag (public % + whitelist). A caller the flag passes is *held out* (never assigned, sees the universe defaults). Distinct from the universe-level holdout.

allocation_pct.description: Share of the (gated) audience allocated to the experiment, in basis points (0–10000 = 0%–100%). `0` = unallocated. Under pooled assignment this is the size of the universe-pool slice claimed. Use `allocation_percent` (0–100) below to think in percent. Immutable while the experiment is running.

allocation_percent.description: Allocation as a **percentage** (0–100, fractional ok). Friendlier alias for `allocation_pct`; converted to basis points server-side (e.g. `50` = 5000 bp). If both are set, `allocation_percent` wins.

reserved_headroom.description: Basis points of this experiment's split kept empty (0–10000) so a new variant can be appended into it while running without reshuffling. Group weights must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom`. Defaults to the universe's `recommended_headroom` when omitted.

salt.description: Hash salt for bucketing. Auto-generated if omitted. Immutable while running.

params.description: **Deprecated** — the universe now owns the config schema (`param_schema`). Retained for back-compat; new experiments should leave this empty and declare params on the universe. Map of param-name → scalar type.

name.description: Group label (e.g. `control`, `treatment_a`). Max 64 chars.

weight.description: Allocation weight in basis points (0–10000). Weights across all groups must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom` (100% minus any reserved tail).

params.description: Per-variant **override map** — a subset of the universe's `param_schema` keys. A key the variant doesn't set inherits the universe default. Unknown keys (not in the universe schema) are rejected.

items.description: One variant. Its `params` may only override keys declared on the universe schema.

groups.description: Two or more variants. Weights must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom`. Existing weights are immutable while running, but a new variant may be appended into the reserved tail.

significance_threshold.description: p-value cutoff used by the analysis pass. Defaults to `0.05`. Values other than 0.05 require Pro plan or higher.

min_runtime_days.description: Minimum days the experiment must run before results are considered conclusive.

min_sample_size.description: Minimum exposures per group before results are considered conclusive.

sequential_testing.description: Enable sequential testing (always-valid p-values). Requires Premium plan or higher.

goal_metric.description: Single goal metric defined inline — either a DSL `query` or an `event` (+`aggregation`/`value`) the server compiles. Attaching one is required before the experiment can be started. The underlying event is auto-created if missing.

guardrail_metrics.description: Up to 10 guardrail metrics defined inline. Each is upserted (event + metric) and attached with role=guardrail.

CreateExperimentRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/experiments`. `name`, `universe`, and `groups` (≥2, weights sum to 10000) required.

name.description: Stable experiment key. Single segment or `folder.name` (a-z, 0-9, `_`/`-`; max 128 chars). Used by SDKs as `Shipeasy.getExperiment(user, '<name>')`. Immutable after create.
Stable experiment key. Single segment or `folder.name` (a-z, 0-9, `_`/`-`; max 128 chars). Used by SDKs as `Shipeasy.getExperiment(user, '<name>')`. Immutable after create.

anyOf.description: User-attribute name used as the bucketing key — e.g. `user_id` (default), `session_id`, `device_id`, or a custom attribute like `company_id` to keep a whole org on one variant. Defaults to `user_id` when omitted.

holdout_gate.description: Per-experiment holdout gate — the name of a `holdout`-type flag, or `null` to clear. A caller the flag passes is held out.

allocation_pct.description: Basis-points allocation (0–10000). Use `allocation_percent` (0–100) for percent. Immutable while the experiment is running.

reserved_headroom.description: Basis points of the split kept empty for appended variants. Group weights must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom`. May be shrunk (never grown into existing weights) while running when appending a variant.

allocation_percent.description: Allocation as a **percentage** (0–100). Friendlier alias for `allocation_pct`; converted to basis points server-side. Wins over `allocation_pct` if both are supplied. Immutable while running.

universe.description: New universe name. Immutable while running. Returns `422` if the universe doesn't exist.

params.description: **Deprecated** — the universe owns the config schema (`param_schema`). Retained for back-compat. Map of param-name → scalar type.

groups.description: Replacement groups. Weights must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom`. Existing weights/values are immutable while running; a new variant may be appended into the reserved tail.

name.description: Group label (e.g. `control`, `treatment_a`). Max 64 chars.

weight.description: Allocation weight in basis points (0–10000). Weights across all groups must sum to `10000 − reserved_headroom`.

params.description: Per-variant **override map** — a subset of the universe's `param_schema` keys. Unknown keys are rejected; unset keys inherit the universe default.

items.description: One variant. Its `params` may only override keys declared on the universe schema.

goal_metric.description: Replaces the goal metric — DSL `query` or `event` (+`aggregation`/`value`) the server compiles (event auto-upserted).

guardrail_metrics.description: Replaces the guardrail set wholesale (event auto-upserted per entry).

UpdateExperimentRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/admin/experiments/{id}`. Partial — only supplied fields change. `allocation_pct`, `salt`, `universe` and existing group weights/values are immutable while running (stop first); a new variant may still be appended into the reserved tail.

status.description: Target status. Allowed transitions: `draft → running`, `running → stopped`, `stopped → archived`, `draft → archived`, and `archived → draft` (restore — only if the experiment never started). Restarting an archived experiment directly is not allowed; restore it to draft first, then start.

metric_id.description: Existing metric id in the project.

role.description: Metric role. `goal` drives the decision, `guardrail` blocks ship if degraded, `secondary` is informational.

min_effect_of_interest.description: Per-experiment override of the metric's `default_min_effect_of_interest` (relative, 0–1) — the smallest change worth acting on for THIS experiment's decision, which depends on the intervention's cost/risk. `null`/omitted falls back to the metric default. For a `guardrail` this is the non-inferiority margin (how large a regression is tolerated); for a `goal` it is the superiority threshold.

metrics.description: Replacement metrics list — replaces the current attachments wholesale.

SetExperimentMetricsRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/experiments/{id}/metrics`. Replaces the experiment's metric attachments wholesale. Returns `422` if any `metric_id` is unknown.

min_effect_of_interest.description: The resolved per-experiment override, or `null` when this attachment inherits the metric's `default_min_effect_of_interest`.

ExperimentResultRow.description: One persisted analysis row (`experiment_results`) for a single metric/group/day slice. Camel-case wire names; nullable statistics are `null` on control rows, empty arms, or when the analysis pass hasn't produced them.

metric.description: Metric name this row was computed for.

groupName.description: Variant (group) label the row belongs to, e.g. `control` or `treatment`.

n.description: Sample size (exposed units) in this group for the slice, or `null` when unknown.

mean.description: Per-unit metric mean in this group, or `null` on empty arms.

variance.description: Per-unit metric variance in this group, or `null` on empty arms.

delta.description: Absolute difference vs. control (metric units). `null` on the control row.

deltaPct.description: Relative difference vs. control, in percent. `null` on the control row.

ci95Low.description: Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval on `delta`, or `null`. Kept for back-compat — prefer `ciLow`/`ciHigh`.

ci95High.description: Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval on `delta`, or `null`. Kept for back-compat — prefer `ciLow`/`ciHigh`.

ci99Low.description: Lower bound of the 99% confidence interval on `delta`, or `null`. Kept for back-compat — prefer `ciLow`/`ciHigh`.

ci99High.description: Upper bound of the 99% confidence interval on `delta`, or `null`. Kept for back-compat — prefer `ciLow`/`ciHigh`.

ciLow.description: Lower bound of the confidence interval at the project's configured `ci_confidence` (not always 95%), or `null`. When both `ciLow`/`ciHigh` are `null`, fall back to `ci95Low`/`ci95High` (pre-migration rows).

ciHigh.description: Upper bound of the confidence interval at the project's configured `ci_confidence` (not always 95%), or `null`. When both `ciLow`/`ciHigh` are `null`, fall back to `ci95Low`/`ci95High` (pre-migration rows).

pValue.description: Two-sided p-value vs. control, or `null` on the control row / empty arms.

expectedN.description: Sample size per arm the power calculation expects for a conclusive read, or `null` when not computed.

realizedMde.description: Realized minimum detectable effect (absolute, in metric units) — the smallest true effect this run could detect at the experiment's significance level and target power GIVEN the observed variance and per-arm sample sizes. A derived OUTPUT, not an input knob; compare it against the metric's minimum effect of interest to judge whether the run is adequately powered. `null` on the control row and on empty arms.

srmPValue.description: p-value of the sample-ratio-mismatch chi-squared test, or `null` when not computed.

msprtSignificant.description: Sequential (mSPRT) significance flag: `1` significant, `0` not, `null` when sequential testing is off or not computed.

OpsItemStatus.description: Lifecycle status of a queue item. The working flow is `open` → `triaged` → `in_progress` → `ready_for_qa` → `resolved` (or `wont_fix`, terminal from any earlier stage). `blocked` marks an item that can't progress until an external dependency clears — a working state a human sets and clears. `ready_for_qa` is what a developer sets once a fix lands; `resolved` is the QA sign-off, normally flipped in the dashboard after verification — set it directly from code only when the fix has been verified end-to-end. `investigating_by_ai` is a system-owned display state — set when the AI agent (Jarvis) picks an item up to investigate, never chosen by a human — so it is shown but not offered as a manual choice. Two human-gated holding states park an item OUT of the work queue until a human promotes it to `open` in the dashboard, so `GET /api/admin/ops` excludes them under `status=all`/default and returns them only when requested as an exact `status`: `pending_approval` is the pre-open approval gate for untriaged inbound (e.g. connector requests filed from a customer's connectors panel) so it never gets auto-implemented — approving = flipping the status to `open`; `triage` is the onboarding-help bucket — questions/errors submitted to the "Stuck in onboarding?" assistant are funnelled into the platform project as `triage` rows so the team can see where people get stuck and follow up, keeping onboarding chatter out of the work queue until a human moves real items to `open`.

OpsItemPriority.description: Triage priority of a queue item. The lowest tier is `nice_to_have` (not `low`) — carried over from the feature-request "importance" scale this priority set replaced.

OpsInvestigationState.description: Where automation has taken an item in the investigation lifecycle — the signal the ops cockpit renders as "what AI did" plus the one action expected of the user. It is **written as a side-effect of the actions that already happen**, never authored on its own: linking a PR sets `pr_ready`, moving to `in_progress` sets `working`, `ready_for_qa` sets `ready_for_qa`, a system/assistant reply sets `question`, resolving clears it, and creation seeds `needs_triage`/`backlog` (by priority) or `investigated`/`detected` for auto-filed error/alert tickets. `triaged_low` marks a low-signal item AI has looked at but that nothing is moving. Absent on rows filed before the field existed — consumers should treat a missing value as "derive from the other fields".

steps.description: Ordered steps the human should take to unblock — self-contained (the human reads only this card, not the agent's transcript), 3–6 steps, each naming the exact file, command, env var, or dashboard page. Renders markdown.

href.description: Dashboard-relative deep link to the related item. `null` is accepted and treated as "no link".

NotifyOpsRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/notifications`. In `title`, `summary`, and each step, reference admin entities inline with tokens — `#42` (feedback item), `@gate:<name>` (alias `@flag:`), `@experiment:<name>` (`@exp:`), `@config:<name>`, `@killswitch:<name>` (`@ks:`), `@metric:<name>`, `@universe:<name>`, `@alert:<name>` — where `<name>` is the entity's immutable name/slug (e.g. `features.checkout`). The dashboard renders each token as a live hover chip deep-linking to the entity; tokens degrade to plain text elsewhere, so they're always safe to use.

AlertApiRow.description:
One FIRED alert — a condition the platform observed and raised, not the rule that defines it (alert *rules* are the `/api/admin/alert-rules` resource). Alerts are never filed by hand: the UI (killswitch flips) and the worker (analysis consumer + alerts cron) raise them, keyed by `(source, dedupeKey)` so re-raising refreshes the active row and a clearing condition auto-resolves it.

Every field is always present on the wire; nullable fields are `null`, never absent.

source.description: Which platform subsystem raised the alert — a metric-threshold rule crossing (`metric_rule`), an armed kill switch (`killswitch_armed`), a sample-ratio mismatch or peeking guard on an experiment (`experiment_srm` / `experiment_peek`), or a guardrail-metric breach (`guardrail`).

ruleId.description: Id of the alert rule that raised it (`ar_…`), for `source: metric_rule`. `null` for alerts raised outside the rules engine.

severity.description: How loud the alert is — `danger` pages, `warn` is the default, `info` is FYI-only.

dedupeKey.description: Idempotency + reconcile key within `(project, source)` — re-raising the same condition refreshes the existing active row instead of stacking duplicates, and the condition clearing flips that row to `resolved`.

detail.description: Longer human-readable explanation — the observed condition, thresholds, and context.

href.description: Dashboard-relative link to the surface that explains the alert (e.g. the experiment or kill switch page), or `null` if there is no single destination.

observedValue.description: The numeric value that tripped the condition (e.g. the metric aggregate a rule compared against its threshold), or `null` when the source has no scalar observation.

status.description: Lifecycle state. `active` means the condition currently holds (shown as "Firing" in the UI); `resolved` means it cleared (auto, when the raiser reconciles, or by hand); `dismissed` means a human waved it off.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the alert was first raised.

resolvedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the alert flipped to `resolved`, or `null` while it hasn't.

dismissedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the alert was dismissed, or `null` while it hasn't been.

assigneeId.description: PERSON owner of the triage — a `users.id` (soft reference, same value space as a feedback item's `assigneeId`), or `null` if unassigned. Set from the ops cockpit's Owner column; independent of the agent half.

assigneeConnectorId.description: AGENT owner — the id of a connected trigger connector (`connectors.id`), or `null`. Mutually exclusive with `assigneeAgent`.

assigneeAgent.description: AGENT owner — a built-in hosted Shipeasy agent (`"jarvis"`, Enterprise-gated), or `null`. Mutually exclusive with `assigneeConnectorId`.

ListAlertsResponse.description: A bare JSON array of fired alerts, ordered by `createdAt` descending. There is no pagination envelope.

UpdateAlertRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /api/admin/alerts/{id}` — the triage writes the ops cockpit needs on a fired alert. All fields optional, at least one required; only the fields present are changed.

Flipping `status` stamps the matching timestamp (`resolvedAt` on `resolved`, `dismissedAt` on `dismissed`; both cleared on `active`). The person owner (`assigneeId`) and the agent owner (`agent`) are independent halves — setting one never touches the other.

status.description: New lifecycle state. `resolved` / `dismissed` stamp their timestamp; `active` re-opens and clears both.

assigneeId.description: PERSON owner — a `users.id`, or `null` to clear the assignment.

agent.description: AGENT owner — a connected trigger connector's id (`connectors.id`), the built-in `"jarvis"` (Enterprise plan only — rejected with `403` otherwise), or `null` to clear. Stored in `assigneeConnectorId` or `assigneeAgent` depending on the value; the two are mutually exclusive.
