ErrorOccurrence.description: One sampled instance behind a tracked error — the minimal per-occurrence payload (what varies between instances; everything else lives on the parent `ErrorRecord`). Every column is always present on the wire; nullable columns are `null`, never absent.

projectId.description: Project this occurrence belongs to. Always echoes the parent error's `projectId` — redundant on this nested read (a projection candidate), kept because the raw row is returned as-is today.

errorId.description: Id of the parent tracked error (`err_…`). Always echoes the enclosing `ErrorRecord.id` — redundant on this nested read (a projection candidate), kept because the raw row is returned as-is today.

message.description: This instance's raw message (unlike the fingerprint, never normalized).

stack.description: This instance's stack trace, or `null` if none was captured.

url.description: This instance's raw URL (ids intact), or `null`.

env.description: Published env this instance ran against, or `null`.

side.description: Which SDK side reported it — `client` or `server`. `null` if unknown.

extrasJson.description: This instance's sanitized extras, JSON-encoded. `null` if none.

sampleRate.description: 1-in-N sampling rate in force when this instance was kept (`1` = the issue was still being recorded exhaustively).

seenAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp this instance was ingested.

ErrorRecord.description:
A tracked production error — one row per distinct issue, keyed by `fingerprint`. Rows are never created by hand: an ingestion path (worker log drain / the `see()` SDK reporter) folds each occurrence into the matching row, bumping `count` and `lastSeenAt`. The admin surface only lists them, reads one, and flips `status`.

Field names are camelCase (the D1 row projected through Drizzle). Every row column is always present on the wire; many are *nullable* because the reporting source may not supply them — `null`, never absent. Only `occurrences` is conditional (detail reads only).

fingerprint.description: Stable dedupe key — the issue TITLE (hash of `errorType` + `subject` + `outcome`, plus the violation name for violations). Unique per project — every occurrence with the same title folds into this one row; per-instance variety (message/stack/extras) lives in `occurrences`.

causedByFingerprint.description: Fingerprint of the issue this one descends from — set when `see()` reported the same error (re-throw) or its `{ cause }` (wrap) at an inner boundary first. Points at another error's `fingerprint` in the same project (soft reference, no FK). `null` for a root issue.

errorType.description: Error class/name, e.g. `TypeError`. `null` when the source didn't supply one.

stack.description: Stack trace of the latest occurrence, or `null` if none was captured.

source.description: Where it surfaced — Worker name (`shipeasy`, `shipeasy-worker`) or `sdk-client` / `sdk-server`. `null` if unknown.

url.description: Latest occurrence's raw URL (with ids intact), or `null`.

seenUrls.description: Distinct, id-normalized route templates this issue has surfaced on, as a JSON-encoded string array (e.g. `["https://app/dashboard/#/gates"]`). UUIDs / numeric ids / opaque tokens are collapsed to `#` so the same route under different ids counts once. `null` if none recorded.

subject.description: Consequence subject — `<errorType> causes the <subject> to <outcome>`. `null` if no consequence was reported.

outcome.description: Consequence outcome — see `subject`. `null` if no consequence was reported.

side.description: Which SDK side reported it — `client` or `server`. `null` if unknown.

env.description: Published env the reporting SDK ran against (e.g. `dev`, `staging`, `prod`). `null` if unknown.

kind.description: `see()` error kind. `null` when the source didn't classify the occurrence.

lastExtrasJson.description: Latest occurrence's sanitized extras, JSON-encoded. `null` if none.

sdkVersion.description: `@shipeasy/sdk` version of the latest occurrence, or `null`.

count.description: EXACT number of folded occurrences for this fingerprint — every occurrence increments it, regardless of detail-row sampling.

occurrences.description: Sampled per-instance detail rows behind this issue, newest first. Returned only by `GET /api/admin/errors/{id}` (never in list responses). The parent row's `count` / `firstSeenAt` / `lastSeenAt` are exact; these rows are a *sampled sketch* of the instances — exhaustive while the issue is small, thinning to roughly 1-in-10 past 10 occurrences, 1-in-100 past 100, and 1-in-1000 past 1000 (each row's `sampleRate` records the rate in force when it was kept), capped at the newest 100 rows.

status.description: Triage state. `open` is the default; a `resolved` error reopens automatically (ingestion-side) if it recurs; `ignored` is sticky until flipped back here.

firstSeenAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the first folded occurrence.

lastSeenAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent folded occurrence. Rows are ordered by this, descending.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the row was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last mutation (e.g. a status flip).

assigneeId.description: PERSON owner of the triage — a `users.id` (soft reference, same value space as a feedback item's `assigneeId`), or `null` if unassigned. Set from the ops cockpit's Owner column; independent of the agent half.

assigneeConnectorId.description: AGENT owner — the id of a connected trigger connector (`connectors.id`), or `null`. Mutually exclusive with `assigneeAgent`.

assigneeAgent.description: AGENT owner — a built-in hosted Shipeasy agent (`"jarvis"`, Enterprise-gated), or `null`. Mutually exclusive with `assigneeConnectorId`.

ListErrorsResponse.description: A bare JSON array of tracked errors, ordered by `lastSeenAt` descending. There is no pagination envelope — `limit` caps the page size. List rows never include `occurrences` — read one error to get them.

UpdateErrorStatusRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/admin/errors/{id}`. The only mutable field is `status` (open ⇄ resolved ⇄ ignored).

status.description: New triage state. `resolved` reopens automatically on recurrence; `ignored` is sticky.

FileErrorTicketResponse.description: The feedback ticket that tracks this error. Idempotent — if an open `error` ticket already tracks this fingerprint (hand- or auto-filed), the existing one is returned instead of creating a duplicate.

number.description: Human-facing per-project ticket number. `null` only on the idempotent dedupe path when the pre-existing ticket is a legacy row created before per-project numbering.

ErrorSeriesRequest.description: Time window for the occurrence series. Bounds are epoch **seconds** (Analytics Engine stores the occurrence timestamp in seconds). `to` must be strictly greater than `from`.

to.description: Window end, epoch seconds (exclusive). Must be greater than `from`.

bucket.description: Bucket width in seconds (60s–86400s/1d). Defaults to `3600` (hourly). Each returned point is floor-aligned to this width.

ErrorSeriesResponse.description: Bucketed occurrence counts for one tracked error (by its fingerprint), plus the Analytics Engine SQL that produced them.

sql.description: The Analytics Engine SQL executed to produce `rows` (echoed for transparency / debugging).

rows.description: Bucketed occurrence series, ordered by `t` ascending.

t.description: Bucket start, epoch seconds, floor-aligned to `bucket`.

v.description: Occurrence count in the bucket (sample-interval weighted).

ConnectorProvider.description: Connector destination. OAuth/app providers (`google_sheets`, `github`, `slack`) dispatch the event payload to an external destination. Trigger providers (`claude_trigger`, `cursor_trigger`, `copilot_trigger`, `jules_trigger`) instead kick a coding-agent run that burns down the operational queue.

ConnectorEvent.description: Lifecycle event a connector subscribes to. `bug.created` fires when a new bug report lands in the feedback inbox; `feature_request.created` fires for a new feature request. A connector dispatches (posts to Slack / appends a Sheets row / files a GitHub Issue) — or, for trigger connectors, auto-fires a coding-agent run — once per subscribed event.

projectId.description: Id of the project the connector belongs to.

name.description: Human-readable connector label shown in the dashboard.

enabled.description: Whether the connector is active. OAuth providers are created `false` and flip to `true` only after the OAuth/config flow completes; trigger providers default to `true` on create. A disabled connector never dispatches and never auto-fires.

events.description: Events this connector is subscribed to. Empty array = no auto-dispatch / no auto-fire (the connector can still be fired/tested manually).

config.description: Provider-specific, non-secret configuration (e.g. a Google Sheets `spreadsheetId`/`sheetTitle`, a Slack channel ref, or a trigger's repo coordinates + routine id). Secrets are never stored here — they live in an encrypted credentials cipher that is never returned by the API.

accountLabel.description: Display label for the connected account / target (e.g. the OAuth account email, or a trigger's idempotency key such as its repo url or routine id). `null` until the connector is authenticated/configured.

hasCredentials.description: Whether an encrypted credentials cipher is stored for this connector (`true` once OAuth/token setup completed). The cipher itself is never serialised — this boolean is the only signal. `false` for a fresh OAuth stub or a tokenless trigger.

lastError.description: Error message from the most recent failed dispatch/fire attempt, or `null` if the last attempt succeeded (or none has run).

lastAttemptAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent dispatch/fire attempt, or `null` if none has run.

lastSuccessAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent successful dispatch/fire, or `null` if none has succeeded. Preserved across later failures.

ConnectorRecord.description: A connector row. The encrypted credentials cipher backing the connector is intentionally never serialised — `hasCredentials` reports only whether one is stored.

ListConnectorsResponse.description: Bare array of every connector in the project (no pagination envelope).

name.description: Human-readable connector label.

events.description: Events to subscribe to. At least one is required.

CreateOAuthConnectorRequest.description: Create an OAuth/app connector stub. The connector is created `enabled: false` with empty `config` and no credentials; the provider's OAuth flow then attaches credentials and enables it.

routineId.description: The Claude Code routine id this connector fires (the id recorded at setup). Idempotency key for the connector.

fireText.description: Optional default prompt sent on a manual fire / when no event text applies.

ClaudeTriggerConfig.description: Non-secret config for a Claude trigger.

name.description: Human-readable connector label.

events.description: Events that auto-fire the routine. Defaults to empty so the trigger does not auto-fire paid runs until events are subscribed.

token.description: The routine's fire bearer token (secret). **Optional** — a tokenless trigger is recorded but not fireable until a token is added later. Encrypted into the credentials cipher; never persisted in `config` or returned.

enabled.description: Whether the trigger is active on create.

CreateClaudeTriggerRequest.description: Create a Claude Code routine trigger in one shot. Idempotent by `config.routineId` — re-creating with the same routine id updates the existing row.

repoUrl.description: Repo the cloud agent runs against, e.g. `https://github.com/owner/repo`. Idempotency key for the connector.

startingRef.description: Optional git ref the run starts from.

projectId.description: Shipeasy project id the run targets.

CursorTriggerConfig.description: Non-secret config for a Cursor trigger.

name.description: Human-readable connector label.

events.description: Events that auto-fire a cold cloud-agent run. Defaults to empty.

apiKey.description: Cursor API key that launches the run (secret). Encrypted into the credentials cipher; never returned.

opsKey.description: Restricted Shipeasy ops key, injected into the run as `SHIPEASY_CLI_TOKEN` via the launch envVars (secret). Encrypted; never returned.

enabled.description: Whether the trigger is active on create.

CreateCursorTriggerRequest.description: Create a Cursor cloud-agent trigger. Both keys are required up front (never tokenless). Idempotent by `config.repoUrl`.

owner.description: GitHub repo owner. Together with `repo` forms the connector's idempotency key (`owner/repo`).

baseRef.description: Optional base ref the agent branches from.

projectId.description: Shipeasy project id the run targets.

CopilotTriggerConfig.description: Non-secret config for a Copilot trigger.

name.description: Human-readable connector label.

events.description: Events that auto-fire a Copilot agent task. Defaults to empty.

token.description: Copilot-licensed user PAT (secret). The ops key lives in the repo's GitHub "Agents" secret store and is never sent through Shipeasy. Encrypted; never returned.

enabled.description: Whether the trigger is active on create.

CreateCopilotTriggerRequest.description: Create a GitHub Copilot coding-agent trigger. Idempotent by `config.owner/config.repo`.

source.description: Jules source for the connected repo, e.g. `sources/github/owner/repo`. Idempotency key for the connector.

startingBranch.description: Optional branch the session starts from.

projectId.description: Shipeasy project id the run targets.

JulesTriggerConfig.description: Non-secret config for a Jules trigger.

name.description: Human-readable connector label.

events.description: Events that auto-fire a Jules session. Defaults to empty.

apiKey.description: Jules API key that launches the session (secret). Encrypted into the credentials cipher; never returned.

opsKey.description: Restricted Shipeasy ops key, embedded in the prompt (Jules exposes no env channel) (secret). Encrypted; never returned.

enabled.description: Whether the trigger is active on create.

CreateJulesTriggerRequest.description: Create a Google Jules trigger. Both keys are required up front. Idempotent by `config.source`.

CreateConnectorRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/connectors`, discriminated on `provider`. The OAuth providers (`google_sheets`/`github`/`slack`) create a disabled stub that is finished via the provider's OAuth flow. The trigger providers (`claude_trigger`/`cursor_trigger`/`copilot_trigger`/`jules_trigger`) arrive config + credentials together and are fireable immediately.

name.description: New connector label.

events.description: Replaces the subscribed-events list wholesale. An empty array unsubscribes the connector from every event (e.g. turning off a trigger's auto-fire).

config.description: Replaces the non-secret config wholesale. Secrets are never set through this endpoint.

UpdateConnectorRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/admin/connectors/{id}`. Partial — only supplied fields change. Array/object fields (`events`, `config`) replace, not merge.

UpdateConnectorResponse.description: Update returns only the id — re-fetch via `GET /api/admin/connectors/{id}` for the full row.

text.description: Optional prompt override for the run. When omitted, each provider falls back to its own default (Claude → the configured `fireText`; the others → their built cold-start prompt).

FireConnectorRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/connectors/{id}/fire`. Entirely optional — an empty body kicks the run with the provider default.

ok.description: Always `true` — a successful fire returns HTTP 200. A dispatch failure returns HTTP 502 with the `Error` envelope, not this body.

FireConnectorResponse.description: Result of a successful trigger fire: `{ ok: true }`. When the downstream dispatch fails the endpoint returns HTTP 502 (see the `Error` response), never `ok: false`.

ok.description: Always `true` — a successful test returns HTTP 200. A dispatch failure returns HTTP 502 with the `Error` envelope, not this body.

issueUrl.description: URL of the artifact the test produced (e.g. the GitHub Issue created by a `github` connector), or `null` when the provider produces no linkable artifact.

TestConnectorResponse.description: Result of a successful connector test: `{ ok: true, issueUrl }`. When the downstream dispatch fails the endpoint returns HTTP 502 (see the `Error` response), never `ok: false`.

name.description: New connector label. Optional — omit to leave it unchanged.

token.description: New fire bearer token (secret). Optional — a blank/omitted token leaves the stored cipher untouched; a supplied one replaces it. Encrypted; never returned.

UpdateClaudeTriggerRequest.description: Per-provider PATCH body for editing an existing `claude_trigger` connector (the dashboard "Triggers" tab). The non-secret `config` is always supplied and replaces the stored config wholesale; `name` and `token` are optional.

name.description: New connector label. Optional — omit to leave it unchanged.

apiKey.description: New Cursor launch key (secret). Optional — omit to leave the stored key untouched. Rotatable independently of `opsKey`. Encrypted; never returned.

opsKey.description: New restricted Shipeasy ops key (secret). Optional — omit to leave the stored key untouched. Rotatable independently of `apiKey`. Encrypted; never returned.

UpdateCursorTriggerRequest.description: Per-provider PATCH body for editing an existing `cursor_trigger` connector. `config` replaces the stored config wholesale; `name` and either credential are optional (a supplied credential rotates just that key).

name.description: New connector label. Optional — omit to leave it unchanged.

token.description: New Copilot-licensed user PAT (secret). Optional — omit to leave the stored cipher untouched. Encrypted; never returned.

UpdateCopilotTriggerRequest.description: Per-provider PATCH body for editing an existing `copilot_trigger` connector. `config` replaces the stored config wholesale; `name` and `token` are optional.

name.description: New connector label. Optional — omit to leave it unchanged.

apiKey.description: New Jules launch key (secret). Optional — omit to leave the stored key untouched. Rotatable independently of `opsKey`. Encrypted; never returned.

opsKey.description: New restricted Shipeasy ops key (secret). Optional — omit to leave the stored key untouched. Rotatable independently of `apiKey`. Encrypted; never returned.

UpdateJulesTriggerRequest.description: Per-provider PATCH body for editing an existing `jules_trigger` connector. `config` replaces the stored config wholesale; `name` and either credential are optional.

UpdateTriggerConnectorRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/admin/connectors/{id}/trigger`, discriminated on `provider`. Edits an existing trigger connector: the non-secret `config` is always supplied and replaces the stored config wholesale; `name` and the credential secret(s) are optional (a supplied secret rotates just that key, a blank/omitted one leaves the stored cipher untouched).

CreateTriggerConnectorRequest.description: Body for `POST /api/admin/connectors/trigger`, discriminated on `provider` — the trigger-provider subset of `CreateConnectorRequest` (no OAuth stubs). Config + credentials arrive together and the connector is usable immediately; creates are idempotent by each provider's natural key (`config.routineId` / `config.repoUrl` / `config.owner`+`config.repo` / `config.source`).

KeyRecord.description: A single API key as returned by the list endpoint. Response fields are snake_case. The raw token is never returned here — only its `last4` tail. `last4` and the typed `scopes` may be `null` for keys minted before those columns existed.

id.description: Stable opaque key id (UUID). Use it to revoke the key.

type.description: Key kind. `server` (back-end, full SDK auth), `client` (public, browser-embeddable), `admin` (CLI/devtools auth token), `ops` (restricted unattended-trigger credential).

env.description: Environment the key is bound to. The worker derives the read env from the key, so this is the isolation boundary. `admin`/`ops` keys are env-agnostic and pinned to `prod`.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the key was minted.

revoked_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the key was revoked, or `null` while the key is active.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry, or `null` for a key that never expires. `admin` keys are fixed at 90 days; `ops` keys use a short sliding window.

created_by_email.description: Email of the human or actor that minted the key, or `null` when not attributable (e.g. CLI device-flow).

name.description: Human label for the key. `null` for unnamed dashboard mints; programmatic mints auto-name themselves.

scopes.description: Permission strings recorded on the key for audit/display, or `null` when none were set.

last4.description: Last 4 characters of the raw token, so the dashboard mask is matchable against the held key. `null` for keys minted before this column.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or `null` on the last page. Pass it back as the `cursor` query parameter.

ListKeysResponse.description: One page of API keys plus a keyset `next_cursor` (ordered `created_at desc, id desc`).

CreateKeyRequest.description:
Body for `POST /api/admin/keys`. Only `type` is required.

`env` is **required** for `server` and `client` keys (the key is bound to one environment — that binding is the read-env isolation boundary). For `admin` and `ops` keys `env` is ignored and the key is pinned to `prod`.

type.description: Key kind to mint. `server` (back-end), `client` (public, browser), `admin` (CLI/devtools token), `ops` (restricted unattended-trigger credential). `admin`/`ops` keys don't count toward the plan key limit.

name.description: Optional human label. Programmatic (API) mints that omit it get an auto-generated descriptive name; dashboard mints may leave it blank.

scopes.description: Optional permission strings recorded on the key. `tickets:public_create` is enforced: a client key needs it (plus the project's `allowPublicTickets` setting) to file a `pending_approval` bug via the public `POST /cli/report` endpoint. The rest are audit/display only.

expiresInDays.description: Days until the key expires (1–3650), or `null`/omitted for a key that never expires. Ignored for `admin` keys (fixed 90-day expiry) and `ops` keys (short sliding window).

env.description: Environment to bind the key to. Always required. For `server`/`client` keys it is the isolation boundary the worker reads from; `admin`/`ops` keys are env-agnostic and pinned to `prod` by the handler regardless of the value sent.

CreateKeyResponse.description: The minted key. The plaintext `key` is returned **once**, here — it is stored hashed and can never be retrieved again.

id.description: Stable opaque key id (UUID). Use it to revoke the key.

type.description: The kind of key that was minted.

env.description: Environment the key is bound to (`prod` for `admin`/`ops`).

key.description: The plaintext API token (e.g. `sdk_server_…`). Returned once — store it now; it cannot be recovered.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry, or `null` if the key never expires.

RevokeKeyResponse.description: Confirms the key is revoked. Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked key returns the same shape.

id.description: Id of the key that was revoked.

SearchHit.description: A single universal-search hit. Field names are camelCase; `title` is `null` when the resource has no human title/display name (members fall back to a role label).

type.description: Resource family — drives the result group + icon in the palette. (`member` hits are surfaced first.)

id.description: Stable identifier used to deep-link into the row's detail pane. For `member` hits this is the teammate's email; for everything else it is the resource id.

name.description: The matched handle — the resource's immutable `name`, or the member's display name (falling back to email).

title.description: Human title/display name when present, else `null`. For `member` hits this carries the email (or the `Owner`/`Member` role when no profile name is set).

href.description: Project-relative href that opens the row (e.g. `/gates?open=<id>`).

SearchResponse.description: Flat, ranked-by-type list of search hits the command palette groups for display. Capped per resource family so one noisy type can't crowd out the rest.

BadRequest.description: The request was malformed (bad JSON or missing project scope).

value.error: Malformed request (bad JSON, missing project scope).

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid admin SDK key.

value.error: Missing or invalid admin SDK key.

Forbidden.description: The key is valid but not allowed to perform this action.

FORBIDDEN.summary: Not an admin of this project

value.error: Key is valid but not allowed to act on this project.

PLAN_REQUIRED.summary: Feature gated behind a higher plan

value.error: The requested feature requires a higher plan tier (e.g. sequential testing, custom alpha, holdout, group count).

NotFound.description: The resource does not exist or is not visible to the caller.

value.error: The resource does not exist or is not visible to the caller.

Conflict.description: The mutation conflicts with current state.

value.error: A resource with this name already exists in the project.
The resource cannot be archived/deleted while another resource still references it.
The requested lifecycle transition is not allowed from the current state.

IMMUTABLE_FIELD.summary: Edited an immutable field while running

value.error: A field that is immutable in the current state was modified (e.g. editing allocation while running).
The request body failed structural (schema) validation.
A referenced entity (universe, metric, gate, event) does not exist.
Experiment group weights must sum to 10000 (basis points).
The referenced event is still pending review and cannot back a metric yet.

BadGateway.description:
The request was well-formed and the resource exists, but a downstream provider
the endpoint delegates to (e.g. a connector's Slack/GitHub/cloud-agent dispatch)
failed. The failure is upstream of this API, not a client error. No stable
`ErrorCode` is assigned to upstream failures, so `code` is omitted.

value.error: GitHub API responded 403: resource not accessible by integration.
